channel.title: LEXOLEN Research Blog Feed
channel.description: Comprehensive security research and technical analysis from LEXOLEN - offensive security, threat intelligence, and red team tooling

item.title: Bypassing EDR with direct syscalls – a practical guide
item.description: A practical walkthrough on bypassing endpoint detection and response solutions using direct syscalls. Covers Nt* function calls, heaven's gate technique, and anti-analysis measures.
item.content:encoded:
Introduction

Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) solutions have become increasingly sophisticated in detecting malicious activity. This post explores advanced evasion techniques using direct system calls to bypass user-mode hooks.

Techniques Covered:

  • Direct syscall invocation
  • Heaven's Gate for WoW64
  • Custom syscall stubs
  • EDR hook evasion

Tools referenced: Custom syscall library, EDR testing framework

item.title: Hunting for Cobalt Strike in encrypted traffic
item.description: Techniques for detecting Cobalt Strike activity hidden within encrypted network traffic. Includes beacon analysis, C2 pattern recognition, and decryption methods.
item.content:encoded:
Beacon Analysis

Cobalt Strike beacons exhibit unique patterns even when encrypted. This analysis covers:

Detection Methods:

  • TLS fingerprinting
  • Jitter analysis
  • HTTP header patterns
  • Certificate validation

Integration with: Custom IDS rules, threat hunting playbooks

item.title: Linux kernel exploit development – heap spraying
item.description: Exploring heap spraying techniques in Linux kernel exploit development. Covers SLUB allocator, kmalloc spraying, and reliable exploitation methods.
item.content:encoded:
SLUB Allocator Deep Dive

The SLUB allocator presents unique challenges and opportunities for heap spraying:

Techniques:

  • kmalloc cache manipulation
  • Object spraying strategies
  • Cache poisoning
  • Reliable overwrite methods

PoC code available in our kernel-exploits repository

item.title: Container escape techniques in Kubernetes environments
item.description: Advanced container escape techniques targeting Kubernetes clusters. Covers privileged containers, hostPath mounts, and API server attacks.
item.content:encoded:
Kubernetes Attack Surface

Kubernetes presents multiple attack vectors for container escapes:

Escape Vectors:

  • Privileged pod exploitation
  • hostPath volume attacks
  • API server misconfigurations
  • Network policy bypasses

Testing methodology for container security assessments

item.title: Reverse engineering Android malware with Ghidra
item.description: Comprehensive guide to reverse engineering Android malware using Ghidra. Covers APK analysis, DEX disassembly, and obfuscation techniques.
item.content:encoded:
Android Malware Analysis Workflow

Systematic approach to Android malware reverse engineering:

Analysis Steps:

  • APK structure examination
  • DEX file disassembly
  • String decryption
  • Dynamic analysis setup

Ghidra scripts and analysis tools included

item.title: Windows privilege escalation via UAC bypass
item.description: Modern User Account Control bypass techniques for Windows privilege escalation. Covers COM hijacking, DLL injection, and elevation methods.
item.content:encoded:
UAC Architecture Overview

Understanding UAC internals for effective bypass development:

Bypass Techniques:

  • COM object hijacking
  • DLL search order exploitation
  • AppInfo service manipulation
  • Token impersonation

PoC implementations for each technique

item.title: CloudTrail log analysis for AWS threat hunting
item.description: Advanced CloudTrail log analysis techniques for detecting AWS threats. Includes anomaly detection, lateral movement identification, and automated alerting.
item.content:encoded:
CloudTrail Threat Hunting

Leveraging AWS CloudTrail for comprehensive threat detection:

Analysis Techniques:

  • API call pattern analysis
  • Geographic anomaly detection
  • Resource enumeration patterns
  • Automated alert generation

Custom Python scripts for CloudTrail analysis

item.title: IoT device exploitation – from firmware to root
item.description: Complete guide to IoT device exploitation from firmware extraction to root shell. Covers embedded device analysis and common vulnerabilities.
item.content:encoded:
IoT Attack Methodology

Systematic approach to IoT device security assessment:

Exploitation Phases:

  • Firmware extraction techniques
  • Embedded system analysis
  • Vulnerability identification
  • Root shell acquisition

Tools: Binwalk, Ghidra, QEMU emulation

item.title: WebAssembly malware analysis and detection
item.description: Analyzing WebAssembly-based malware and developing detection signatures. Covers WASM bytecode analysis and runtime behavior monitoring.
item.content:encoded:
WebAssembly Threat Landscape

WASM provides new attack vectors for browser-based malware:

Analysis Techniques:

  • WASM bytecode disassembly
  • Runtime behavior analysis
  • Signature development
  • Browser sandbox escapes

Custom WASM analysis tools and signatures

item.title: Active Directory attack path mapping with BloodHound
item.description: Advanced BloodHound usage for mapping Active Directory attack paths. Covers custom queries, data collection, and exploitation strategies.
item.content:encoded:
BloodHound Advanced Techniques

Maximizing BloodHound for red team operations:

Analysis Features:

  • Custom Cypher queries
  • Attack path visualization
  • Data collection optimization
  • Automated exploitation

Integration with our BloodHound-CE fork

item.title: Cryptographic attacks on TLS 1.3 implementations
item.description: Cryptographic attacks targeting TLS 1.3 implementations. Covers key exchange weaknesses, implementation flaws, and downgrade attacks.
item.content:encoded:
TLS 1.3 Security Analysis

Examining TLS 1.3 for cryptographic weaknesses:

Attack Vectors:

  • Key exchange manipulation
  • Implementation flaws
  • Downgrade attack prevention
  • Quantum resistance assessment

Custom TLS testing tools and research findings

item.title: Memory forensics in Windows crash dumps
item.description: Advanced memory forensics techniques using Windows crash dumps. Covers Volatility framework usage and custom plugin development.
item.content:encoded:
Windows Memory Analysis

Leveraging crash dumps for comprehensive memory forensics:

Analysis Techniques:

  • Volatility plugin development
  • Process memory reconstruction
  • Malware artifact extraction
  • Timeline reconstruction

Custom Volatility plugins included

item.title: Bluetooth Low Energy security research
item.description: Security research on Bluetooth Low Energy protocols. Covers pairing attacks, man-in-the-middle techniques, and firmware analysis.
item.content:encoded:
Bluetooth LE Threat Model

Comprehensive analysis of BLE security mechanisms:

Research Areas:

  • Pairing protocol weaknesses
  • MITM attack vectors
  • Firmware reverse engineering
  • Privacy implications

Custom BLE testing tools and research papers

item.title: Automated exploit generation with symbolic execution
item.description: Using symbolic execution engines for automated exploit generation. Covers angr, Manticore, and custom symbolic execution frameworks.
item.content:encoded:
Symbolic Execution for Exploits

Automated vulnerability discovery and exploit generation:

Technologies:

  • angr framework integration
  • Constraint solving
  • Path exploration
  • Exploit synthesis

PoC exploit generation scripts

item.title: Supply chain attack techniques and detection
item.description: Analysis of supply chain attack methodologies and detection strategies. Covers dependency confusion, build system compromises, and mitigation techniques.
item.content:encoded:
Supply Chain Threat Landscape

Understanding and defending against supply chain attacks:

Attack Techniques:

  • Dependency confusion
  • Build pipeline compromise
  • Third-party library injection
  • Watering hole attacks

Detection tools and security best practices

item.title: macOS kernel extension exploitation
item.description: Exploiting macOS kernel extensions for privilege escalation. Covers IOKit drivers, Mach ports, and XNU kernel vulnerabilities.
item.content:encoded:
macOS Kernel Exploitation

Targeting macOS kernel extensions for elevated privileges:

Exploit Vectors:

  • IOKit driver vulnerabilities
  • Mach port manipulation
  • XNU kernel bugs
  • SIP bypass techniques

macOS-specific exploit development tools

item.title: AI/ML model poisoning attacks and defenses
item.description: Research on poisoning attacks against AI/ML models and defense mechanisms. Covers data poisoning, model inversion, and adversarial training.
item.content:encoded:
AI/ML Security Research

Attacking and defending machine learning systems:

Research Areas:

  • Data poisoning techniques
  • Model inversion attacks
  • Adversarial input generation
  • Defense mechanisms

Custom ML security testing tools

item.title: Network protocol fuzzing with custom mutators
item.description: Advanced network protocol fuzzing techniques with custom mutation engines. Covers stateful fuzzing, grammar-based mutations, and coverage-guided approaches.
item.content:encoded:
Protocol Fuzzing Methodology

Comprehensive approach to network protocol security testing:

Fuzzing Techniques:

  • Stateful protocol modeling
  • Grammar-based mutations
  • Coverage-guided fuzzing
  • Custom mutator development

Integration with our AutoRecon network scanner

item.title: Hardware-assisted virtualization rootkits
item.description: Developing rootkits using hardware-assisted virtualization. Covers Intel VT-x, AMD-V, and hypervisor-based stealth techniques.
item.content:encoded:
Hardware Virtualization Rootkits

Leveraging CPU virtualization extensions for advanced rootkits:

Implementation Techniques:

  • VT-x/AMD-V exploitation
  • Hypervisor development
  • Stealth mechanisms
  • Detection evasion

PoC hypervisor rootkit implementation

item.title: Zero-day vulnerability research in popular libraries
item.description: Systematic zero-day vulnerability research methodology applied to popular open-source libraries. Covers fuzzing, code review, and exploit development.
item.content:encoded:
Zero-Day Research Methodology

Structured approach to discovering unknown vulnerabilities:

Research Process:

  • Target library selection
  • Source code analysis
  • Fuzzing campaign setup
  • Vulnerability validation

Responsible disclosure guidelines and case studies

item.title: Blockchain smart contract security analysis
item.description: Security analysis of blockchain smart contracts. Covers Solidity vulnerabilities, DeFi protocol attacks, and automated contract auditing tools.
item.content:encoded:
Smart Contract Vulnerabilities

Common security issues in blockchain smart contracts:

Vulnerability Classes:

  • Reentrancy attacks
  • Integer overflow/underflow
  • Access control flaws
  • Oracle manipulation

Custom smart contract analysis tools

item.title: Advanced persistent threat simulation framework
item.description: Framework for simulating advanced persistent threat campaigns. Covers multi-stage attacks, C2 infrastructure, and long-term persistence techniques.
item.content:encoded:
APT Simulation Framework

Comprehensive framework for red team APT simulations:

Framework Components:

  • Multi-stage attack chains
  • C2 infrastructure setup
  • Persistence mechanisms
  • Anti-forensic techniques

Modular APT simulation toolkit
